data.title: Wall Street analysts predict Flux Power to report Q4 loss of 8 cents/share, revenue $16.47M, with a recent $1.2M+ order. Stock rose 6.5% to $1.80.
data.description: Flux Power Holdings, Inc. (NASDAQ: FLUX) is set to announce its fourth-quarter earnings after markets close on Tuesday, Sept. 16. Analysts predict a quarterly loss of 8 cents per share, an improvement from the 16 cents loss per share reported a year ago. Revenue for the quarter is anticipated to be around $16.47 million, up from $13.23 million in the same period last year. In recent news, the company secured an additional order of over $1.2 million from a major North American airline. Following this announcement, Flux Power shares climbed by 6.5% to reach a closing price of $1.80 on Thursday. Details about the latest analyst ratings can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: NEAR/USDT price at $2.70, down 0.77%, amid cross-chain growth to 20 blockchains. RSI neutral at 57.77.
data.description: The price of NEAR remains stable at $2.70 even though it experienced a slight daily decline of 0.77%. This comes as the protocol is expanding its reach to include 20 blockchains. Analysis of technical indicators reveals mixed signals, with the Relative Strength Index (RSI) currently at a neutral value of 57.77.

data.title: Experts recommend focusing on bonds, large-cap growth stocks, and real assets ahead of expected Fed rate cut on September 16-17. Bond yields dropping, with 10-year Treasury yielding 4.05%.
data.description: Market expert John Murillo, Chief Business Officer at B2BROKER, suggests that with an impending interest rate cut by the U.S. Federal Reserve, investors should focus on three key asset classes: bonds, large-cap growth stocks, and real assets. The recommendation comes ahead of the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meeting on September 16–17, where a 25-basis-point rate cut is anticipated. Murillo advises investors to consider moving extra cash into solid fixed-income assets to capitalize on yields before they potentially decrease further. U.S. Treasury bond yields have already been dropping, with the 10-year Treasury yielding 4.05%. For more details, the full story is available on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Blue Hat BHAT completed over 123KG ($13.3M) in gold trading H1 2025, aiming for 550KG total FY 2025.
data.description: On September 12, 2025, Blue Hat Interactive Entertainment Technology Inc. (BHAT), a company listed on NASDAQ, revealed that it successfully concluded gold trading worth over 123 kilograms, equivalent to US$13,326,754, in the initial six months of 2025. The company forecasts that the trading volume could potentially surge to 550 kilograms by the end of fiscal year 2025, contingent upon market dynamics.

data.title: Apple unveiled iPhone 17 with new design and analysts downgraded stock; investors eye gain from dividends.
data.description: During the "Awe Dropping" event on Tuesday, Apple Inc. revealed its latest iPhone 17 lineup, featuring a revamped iPhone 17 Pro lineup that now utilizes precision-machined aluminum instead of titanium. The new design includes a colorful oxide layer and a full-width camera plateau housing antennas. Following the event, Phillip Securities analyst Helena Wang downgraded Apple's rating from Neutral to Reduce with a $200 price target. DA Davidson analyst Gil Luria also downgraded the stock from Buy to Neutral, maintaining a price target of $250. Investors are interested in potential dividends, as Apple currently offers an annual dividend yield of 0.45%, paid semi-annually.

data.title: XLM surpasses $0.39 fueled by US gov adoption news, trading up 0.03% daily. Technicals indicate bullish momentum.
data.description: The cryptocurrency XLM is currently trading at $0.39, representing a daily increase of 0.03%. Technical analysis suggests a bullish trend, likely influenced by recent news of adoption by the U.S. Department of Commerce. This development has generated positive momentum for XLM in the market.

data.title: BorgWarner recognized on TIME's 2025 World's Best Companies List in collaboration with Statista, a leading data provider.
data.description: In September 12, 2025, Auburn Hills, Michigan, BorgWarner was named to TIME's prestigious World's Best Companies 2025 list. This recognition was achieved with the help of Statista, a top global provider of market and consumer data and rankings. The award acknowledges BorgWarner's excellence in the industry.

data.title: Powerfleet (Nasdaq: AIOT) to attend Barrington Research Virtual Fall Investment Conference on Sep 16th.
data.description: Located in Woodcliff Lake, New Jersey, on September 12, 2025, Powerfleet, Inc. (Nasdaq: AIOT) shared plans to participate in the Barrington Research Virtual Fall Investment Conference on September 16th. The company's management will be present at the conference to engage with investors regarding Powerfleet's AIoT platform. This event provides an opportunity for Powerfleet to discuss its strategies and growth prospects with interested parties.

data.title: Workdry International acquires Atlas Dewatering in Florida to strengthen North American rental business.
data.description: In West Palm Beach, Florida, as of September 12, 2025, Workdry International, the parent company of leading pump rental and wastewater treatment providers including Holland Pump, Selwood, Siltbuster, and Vanderkamp, has successfully finalized the acquisition of Atlas Dewatering. This strategic move further solidifies Workdry's position in the industry, expanding its portfolio to enhance services and solutions for its customers.

data.title: LakeShore Biopharma receives delisting determination letter from Nasdaq on September 12, 2025.
data.description: On September 12, 2025, LakeShore Biopharma Co., Ltd (Nasdaq: LSB) announced its commitment to advancing vaccines and therapeutic biologics globally. The company focuses on the discovery, development, manufacturing, and distribution of innovative medical solutions.

data.title: Timeline rebrands its skincare portfolio to align with scientific advancements, focusing on holistic wellness. September 12, 2025.
data.description: In New York on September 12, 2025, Timeline, a leading company in cellular longevity and mitochondrial health, revealed a major skincare rebranding initiative. The rebrand encompasses a revamped identity and a new skincare lineup. This marks a significant transformation for the company as it moves towards a more innovative approach in the skincare sector.

data.title: Rita's and Abbott Elementary launch mango ice, offer Extra Credit Wednesdays through Oct 15 in Philly. Celebrating new season of Abbott Elementary.
data.description: To mark the return of the popular TV show Abbott Elementary on ABC, Rita's Italian Ice is introducing a special new flavor for a limited time. Additionally, they are offering Extra Credit Wednesdays until October 15th. This exciting promotion is taking place in Philadelphia and coincides with the new season of the show. Don't miss out on this opportunity to enjoy delicious Italian Ice while tuning in to the latest episodes of Abbott Elementary.
